risks: No NSG resources defined — source VM and public IPs are exposed without network security controls, and ASR requires outbound connectivity rules via service tags
Traffic Manager health probe uses HTTP on port 80 instead of HTTPS — monitoring traffic is unencrypted
No monitoring or diagnostics resources (Log Analytics workspace, diagnostic settings) for operational visibility into DR events
ASR fabric, protection container, and replication protected item resources are absent — replication will not be active until these are configured post-deployment
No managed identity assigned to the Automation Account — failback runbooks may require manual credential management
Automation Account has no runbook definitions — failback orchestration logic is not yet codified in the plan
correctionsRecommended: Add NSG resources for both source and target subnets with outbound rules allowing ASR service tags (Storage, AzureSiteRecovery, AzureActiveDirectory, EventHub)
Switch Traffic Manager monitor_config protocol from HTTP to HTTPS (port 443) for encrypted health probing
Add a Log Analytics workspace and configure diagnostic settings on the Recovery Services Vault, Traffic Manager profile, and Automation Account
Add ASR fabric and protection container resources to complete the replication topology from East US to West US
Assign a system-managed identity to the Automation Account for secure, credential-free access to Azure resources during failback
Add resource tags (e.g., environment, cost-center, dr-role) across all resources for operational and cost management
Consider adding a Network Interface (NIC) resource explicitly for the VM to clarify network attachment
